data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me wait for the agent to complete and then synthesize the findings.

Key issues I can see:

1. **Duplicated properties across all 5 projects**: TargetFramework, LangVersion, Nullable, ImplicitUsings, TreatWarningsAsErrors, EnforceCodeStyleInBuild, Company, Authors - all repeated identically in every project.

2. **No Directory.Build.props** - all common properties should be centralized there.

3. **No Directory.Packages.props** - package versions are scattered and inconsistent:
   - `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` is 8.0.0 in Core and Api, but 8.0.1 in Services (version drift!)
   - Test packages also have drift: `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` ranges from 17.9.0 to 17.10.0, `xunit` from 2.7.1 to 2.8.1, and `xunit.runner.visualstudio` from 2.5.6 to 2.8.1

4. **Duplicated custom target** - `PrintBuildInfo` target is copy-pasted in all 3 src projects. Should go in Directory.Build.targets.

5. **Duplicated DocumentationFile property** - same in all 3 src projects.

6. **Test projects duplicate test infrastructure** - similar setup repeated across test projects.

7. **No Directory.Build.targets** - missing the centralized file that would consolidate these shared configurations.

Let me wait for the agent to finish scanning for any other issues.

data.content:
I now have a complete picture of all 5 project files and the solution infrastructure. There are no `Directory.Build.props`, `Directory.Build.targets`, `Directory.Packages.props`, `.editorconfig`, or `global.json` files present. Let me deliver the full review.

---

# 🔍 MSBuild Project File Review — ContosoApp Solution

## Solution Overview

| File | SDK | TFM | Packages |
|------|-----|-----|----------|
| `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | net8.0 | 2 |
| `ContosoApp.Services.csproj` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | net8.0 | 2 (+1 ProjectRef) |
| `ContosoApp.Api.csproj` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web | net8.0 | 3 (+2 ProjectRef) |
| `ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | net8.0 | 4 (+1 ProjectRef) |
| `ContosoApp.Api.Tests.csproj` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | net8.0 | 4 (+1 ProjectRef) |

**Infrastructure files:** ❌ No `Directory.Build.props` · ❌ No `Directory.Build.targets` · ❌ No `Directory.Packages.props` · ❌ No `.editorconfig` · ❌ No `global.json`

---

## 🔴 Errors (likely to cause real problems)

### E1: Package version drift — `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`

The same package is referenced at **different versions** across projects:

| Project | Version |
|---------|---------|
| `ContosoApp.Core` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | **8.0.1** |
| `ContosoApp.Api` | **8.0.0** |

This is the kind of silent inconsistency that causes NuGet restore to emit **NU1605 (package downgrade)** warnings or pick an unexpected version at runtime. Since `Services` references `Core` (which brings in 8.0.0 transitively), NuGet must reconcile 8.0.0 vs 8.0.1 — and the result depends on resolution strategy.

### E2: Test infrastructure version skew between test projects

The two test projects use **different versions** of every test package:

| Package | `Core.Tests` | `Api.Tests` |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |

This means your two test projects run on different test runner infrastructure. Tests could behave differently, and CI results may not be comparable. Test packages should always be pinned to identical versions across all test projects.

---

## 🟡 Warnings (anti-patterns that should be fixed)

### W1: Massive property duplication across all 5 projects (→ add `Directory.Build.props`)

**All 5 projects** repeat this identical 8-property block:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

That's **40 duplicated lines** (8 properties × 5 files). When you need to change the TFM to `net9.0`, you must edit all 5 files — and inevitably someone will miss one.

**Fix:** Create a `Directory.Build.props` at the repo root:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props (repo root) -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Then remove those 8 lines from every `.csproj`.

### W2: No Central Package Management (→ add `Directory.Packages.props`)

There are **15 PackageReference items** across 5 projects with versions scattered everywhere. This is the #1 cause of the version drift found in E1 and E2.

**Fix:** Create `Directory.Packages.props` at the repo root:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Packages.props (repo root) -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Test infrastructure -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then strip `Version=` from every `<PackageReference>` in every `.csproj`:
```xml
<!-- BEFORE -->
<PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />

<!-- AFTER -->
<PackageReference Include="xunit" />
```

### W3: Duplicated `DocumentationFile` property across 3 source projects

All three source projects repeat:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

This is also in a **second, unnecessary `<PropertyGroup>`** in each file — all properties can go in one group.

**Fix:** Add to `Directory.Build.props` (conditionally for source projects):
```xml
<!-- In Directory.Build.props — only for non-test projects -->
<PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

This is also in a **second, unnecessary `<PropertyGroup>`** in each file — all properties can go in one group.

**Fix:** Add to `Directory.Build.props` (conditionally for source projects):
```xml

  <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

> **Note:** Use `<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>` instead of manually setting `DocumentationFile`. The SDK handles the path automatically — the hardcoded `$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml` is a **restated SDK default**.

### W4: Duplicated `PrintBuildInfo` custom target across 3 source projects (→ use `Directory.Build.targets`)

All three source projects contain this identical target:
```xml

  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

**Fix:** Move to `Directory.Build.targets`:
```xml

<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

This applies to all projects automatically. If you want to exclude test projects, add a `Condition`:
```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
        Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
```

### W5: `PrintBuildInfo` target has no `Inputs`/`Outputs`

The target runs on **every build**, even when nothing has changed. While it only emits a message (so it's fast), the absence of `Inputs`/`Outputs` is a code smell that breaks incremental build correctness patterns. For a diagnostic message this is tolerable, but be careful not to add heavier work to this target without adding I/O tracking.

### W6: No `global.json` — SDK version not pinned

Without a `global.json`, any developer (or CI machine) will use whatever .NET SDK happens to be installed. This can lead to "works on my machine" issues when SDK versions differ.

**Fix:** Add at the repo root:
```json
{
  "sdk": {
    "version": "8.0.400",
    "rollForward": "latestFeature"
  }
}
```

Since `<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>` is set, having a root `.editorconfig` would let you actually define the code style rules that are enforced. Without it, the property is enabled but there are no custom rules to enforce beyond the defaults.
